skill.name: app-store-optimization
skill.description: Complete App Store Optimization toolkit for researching keywords, optimizing metadata, analyzing competitors, and planning launches for Apple App Store and Google Play.
skill.summary: Plan and improve App Store Optimization for mobile apps across keywords, metadata, reviews, localization, testing, and launches.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 431 potential findings, including weak cryptography, shell execution, filesystem, network, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from ASO terminology, markdown code fences, documented installation paths, and local feature request variables. No prompt injection, malicious intent, credential access, command execution, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches ASO Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match words such as description, optimization, confidence, and keyword-related scoring text. These lines implement ASO metadata and A/B testing logic, not cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive hashing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged lines are ASO descriptions, statistical confidence labels, or metadata helpers. No crypto imports, digest calls, cipher use, or password handling were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are markdown code fences and usage examples. The only shell-like content is user-facing installation or verification documentation, not code that runs automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced markdown examples. No subprocess, os.system, eval, exec, Ruby backticks, or shell invocation code was found in the Python modules.

low_findings.title: Low Concern: Documented Hidden Skill Paths
low_findings.description: The filesystem alerts refer to .claude skill installation paths in README examples. This is expected installation guidance and does not access files at runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are documentation for installing the skill into Claude skill directories. No code reads, writes, deletes, or enumerates hidden user files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Feature Request Variables Flagged As Network Use
low_findings.description: The network alerts match local variable and method names containing request. The review analyzer processes review text in memory and does not import HTTP client libraries or call external services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code appends, clusters, and summarizes feature request data from local review objects. Search found no requests, urllib, socket, fetch, or other network client usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms Are Launch Planning Text
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts match legitimate ASO planning terms such as Android versions, support email setup, and platform update monitoring. They do not collect host, network, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is checklist and limitations content for app store planning. No system discovery commands, environment probing, or network scanning behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Mobile teams often struggle to choose keywords, metadata, and launch priorities for crowded app stores. This skill provides structured ASO analysis, optimization guidance, and planning support for Apple App Store and Google Play.
actual_capabilities: Researches app keywords using relevance, competition, and opportunity scoring.
Creates Apple App Store and Google Play metadata with character limit checks.
Analyzes competitor positioning, keyword overlap, ratings, and visible ASO gaps.
Calculates ASO health scores across metadata, ratings, keywords, and conversion metrics.
Plans A/B tests for icons, screenshots, titles, and descriptions with sample size guidance.
Generates launch checklists, localization priorities, and review response templates.
limitations: It does not fetch live App Store or Google Play ranking data by itself.
It does not submit listings or make changes in App Store Connect or Play Console.
Search volume and competitor estimates need validation with current market data.
Store policies change, so final submissions should be checked against current rules.

use_cases.title: Prepare a New App Launch
use_cases.description: Build a keyword plan, metadata package, and pre-launch checklist before submitting a new app.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Listing
use_cases.description: Review current metadata, ratings, conversion metrics, and keyword coverage to prioritize ASO improvements.

use_cases.title: Plan Growth Experiments
use_cases.description: Design A/B tests, compare competitors, and identify localization markets for growth campaigns.

prompt_templates.title: Find Starter Keywords
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner keyword research for a new app idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the app-store-optimization skill to research keywords for my app. My app is in [category], helps [target audience], and has these features: [features].

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Store Metadata
prompt_templates.scenario: Create platform-specific title, subtitle, and description options.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the app-store-optimization skill to optimize my metadata for [Apple App Store or Google Play]. My app name is [name], my target keywords are [keywords], and my current description is [description].

prompt_templates.title: Compare Competitors
prompt_templates.scenario: Analyze ASO gaps against named competitors.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the app-store-optimization skill to compare my app with [competitor names]. Focus on keyword positioning, metadata strategy, reviews, ratings, and opportunities I can use.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full ASO Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning across launch, testing, reviews, and localization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the app-store-optimization skill to create a complete ASO plan for [app name]. Include keyword priorities, metadata changes, A/B tests, review insights, launch checklist, and localization recommendations.

output_examples.input: Research keywords for a productivity app for remote teams.
output_examples.output: The skill returns prioritized keyword groups, competition notes, long-tail opportunities, and recommendations for title, subtitle, and description placement.

output_examples.input: Optimize metadata for a fitness app on Apple App Store.
output_examples.output: The skill returns title and subtitle options, keyword field suggestions, full description guidance, character counts, and readability notes.

output_examples.input: Plan an A/B test for a new screenshot set.
output_examples.output: The skill returns the test hypothesis, traffic split, primary metric, minimum sample size, expected duration, and decision criteria.

best_practices: Provide the app category, audience, current metadata, target markets, and competitors before requesting analysis.
Validate recommendations with current store analytics, search data, and platform policy updates.
Prioritize one measurable ASO change at a time when testing metadata or visual assets.
anti_patterns: Do not stuff keywords into titles or descriptions when readability suffers.
Do not treat Apple App Store and Google Play metadata rules as interchangeable.
Do not rely on one launch audit without ongoing measurement and iteration.

faq.question: Does this skill use live app store data?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured analysis and estimates from the information you provide. Verify important decisions with current store analytics.

faq.question: Can it optimize for both Apple App Store and Google Play?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes separate character limits, metadata fields, and guidance for both major stores.

faq.question: Can it submit my app listing?
faq.answer: No. It prepares recommendations and checklists. You still submit changes through App Store Connect or Google Play Console.

faq.question: What inputs produce the best results?
faq.answer: Share your app name, category, audience, features, current metadata, competitors, ratings, and target markets.

faq.question: Can it help with reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize review sentiment, identify common issues, group feature requests, and draft response templates.

faq.question: How often should ASO be reviewed?
faq.answer: Review ASO after major releases, seasonal shifts, competitor changes, and at least once each quarter.
